title: Slow Clients, Resource Limits, Backpressure, Admission, and Cleanup · Go Build Room
summary: A protocol pairing room predicts byte, parser, state, ownership, and cleanup transitions before assembling the first connection resource budget and overload policy for slow clients, resource limits, backpressure, admission, and cleanup. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of connection resource budget and overload policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Bound start-line, header, content, trailer, idle, and total-phase time using both progress and absolute budgets.
Bound per-connection buffers, retained slices, field maps, content queues, trailer data, and pooled-object capacity before allocation.
Propagate slow peer writes through bounded queues, cancellation, deadlines, partial output evidence, and producer shutdown.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Slow Header Body Defense
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop protocol team handles case 1309 in a protocol upgrade gateway transferring buffered tunnel bytes. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine absolute phase time, memory, and output-queue budgets for slow-peer defense; preserve this operating constraint: no malformed framing may be normalized and forwarded to another recipient; then minimize a fuzz failure and add it to the deterministic regression corpus. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Slow Header Body Defense.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for bound start-line, header, content, trailer, idle, and total-phase time using both progress and absolute budgets. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Slow Header Body Defense, keep Slow Header Body Defense connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

Build deterministic Go evidence to combine absolute phase time, memory, and output-queue budgets for slow-peer defense; preserve this operating constraint: no malformed framing may be normalized and forwarded to another recipient; then split every structural delimiter across a different read and retain the same result. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove connection, goroutine, timer, buffer, queue, tunnel, temporary data, and metric-cardinality cleanup for every terminal state.

Build deterministic Go evidence to combine absolute phase time, memory, and output-queue budgets for slow-peer defense; preserve this operating constraint: no malformed framing may be normalized and forwarded to another recipient; then split every structural delimiter across a different read and retain the same result. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Prove connection, goroutine, timer, buffer, queue, tunnel, temporary data, and metric-cardinality cleanup for every terminal state.
